title: Reel
summary: Anonymous FTP leaks an email from a user asking for RTF files. Weaponize CVE-2017-0199 into a remote HTA payload, relay it through SMTP, catch a shell when the user opens it, decrypt a DPAPI-protected cred.xml for lateral movement, then ride a BloodHound ACL chain (WriteOwner → WriteDACL) to DCSync the domain.

title: Sauna
summary: Scrape usernames from the company website, AS-REP roast the domain, crack fsmith's hash, log in over WinRM, harvest autologon creds from the Winlogon registry key, then DCSync as svc_loanmgr for the Administrator hash.

title: Nibbles
summary: An HTML comment on the landing page hints at a hidden /nibbleblog/ install. Nibbleblog 4.0.3 admin auth + an arbitrary file upload in the My Image plugin yields a PHP shell; sudo then lets the user run a world-writable script as root.

title: Lame
summary: The canonical 'my first HTB box'. Samba 3.0.20 usermap_script command injection (CVE-2007-2447) via a crafted smbclient username gives a straight root shell — no user, no privesc, no pivot.

title: Daily Bugle
summary: Joomla 3.7.0 SQL injection to dump a bcrypt hash, crack it, abuse the admin template editor for RCE, then pivot via config-file password reuse and escalate with sudo yum GTFOBins.

title: Skynet
summary: Multi-vector box: anonymous SMB yields a password wordlist, hydra cracks SquirrelMail, emails reveal a second SMB share with a hidden URL path, Cuppa CMS RFI lands a shell, and a tar wildcard injection on a root cron finishes it.

title: Wonderland
summary: Alice-themed privesc chain: directory traversal to find credentials, a Python module hijack through sudo, a PATH hijack against an SUID binary, and finally cap_setuid on perl for the root shell.

title: Mr Robot CTF
summary: The iconic Mr Robot box: find a self-served wordlist via robots.txt, brute-force WordPress, drop a PHP reverse shell into the 404 template, crack an MD5 hash, and escape via SUID nmap's legacy interactive mode.
